decisions.decision_text: Adopt Approach B — agent + thick methodology skill mirroring klaus + debugging shape; 7-phase argument-audit skill with sparring as terminal phase
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; selected from option list). Recommendation reasoning: B matches the plugin's established klaus.md + debugging shape and is the path of least surprise; A under-delivers on success criteria 1 (non-obvious weaknesses) and 4 (clean round-trip) because both require methodology beyond delegation; C's split between audit and sparring skills creates discovery friction without distinct workflows since sparring is naturally the terminal phase of an audit, not a parallel concern.

decisions.decision_text: Finalise plan after absorbing Klaus's hits #1, #4, #6, #7; conscious non-application of hits #2 and #5
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; selected from option list). Klaus reviewer verdict (verbatim summary): scored 3-0 with seven distinct hits; identified killer (one-shot agents don't loop, breaking sparring assumption), big (SC-9 contradiction-vs-equivalence scoping bug), and four notable/smaller methodology refinements. Revisions absorbed: (#7) sparring split into digest-default + --interactive opt-in via new commands/aron-spar.md follow-up command (adds T9, brings total to 9 tasks); (#1) SC-9 reworded to "shared claim asserted in one doc and attacked in another (literal-text match)"; (#4) skill split from 7 phases to 8 (classify-schemes and fire-critical-questions separated); (#6) SC-7 reworded to "traceable source-span in report; prose may paraphrase". Conscious non-applications: (#2) Walton scope kept at four named schemes; (#5) fixture realism kept as 3 synthetic. Plan locked: 9 tasks across 4 batches; 11 success criteria (SC-1 through SC-10 plus SC-8b); 9 risks (R-1 to R-9 with R-2 impact downgraded Medium→Low after SC-9 rescoping).

decisions.decision_text: Merge each branch separately with --no-ff (preserve history) — three merge commits, one per task
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; selected from option list). Conflict check: None detected (T1 owns argdown-ast-fixture.md, T2 owns three reference docs, T3 owns 10 fixture files; non-overlapping). Three merges executed: worktree-agent-a115f2293a707692c (T1, +970 lines argdown-ast-fixture.md), worktree-agent-acb25fb998378b8f7 (T2, +957 lines across walton-schemes/methodology-references/audit-report-template), worktree-agent-ae95ba9c5988d377e (T3, +547 lines across 10 fixture files). Per-branch history preserved including T3 retry fde13ce and the three fix-up commits, supporting future review-by-blame.
decisions.notes: No keywords matched; catch-all to TRADEOFFS at 0.3.

decisions.decision_text: Merge each branch separately with --no-ff (preserve history) — two merge commits, T4 then T5
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; methodology default after batch 1 set precedent). Conflict check: None detected (T4 owns skills/argument-audit/SKILL.md, T5 owns agents/aron.md; non-overlapping). Two merges executed: worktree-agent-ad2ca396d8532a159 (T4, +259 lines SKILL.md), worktree-agent-a4677a7b4fdf84fec (T5, +241 lines agents/aron.md including the --sober fix-up commit 0d6a8ca on top of 8d0d074).
decisions.notes: No keywords matched; catch-all to TRADEOFFS at 0.3.

decisions.decision_text: Merge each branch separately with --no-ff (preserve history) — two merge commits, T6 then T9
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; methodology default after batch 1/2 precedent). Conflict check: None detected (T6 owns commands/aron.md, T9 owns commands/aron-spar.md; non-overlapping). Two merges: worktree-agent-af19affe8e9021c20 (T6, +148 lines), worktree-agent-a77ec2918dbf093bb (T9, +157 lines including fix-up commit 0a9458e on top of b1f9488).
decisions.notes: No keywords matched; catch-all to TRADEOFFS at 0.3.

decisions.decision_text: Merge T7 README branch with --no-ff (consistent with batches 1-3 precedent). T8a structural verify produced no files to merge. T8b smoke (Klaus-as-Aron) produced no files to merge. SKILL.md v1.0.1 fix-up (gaps 1, 5, 6) committed directly on main as 702bc24 because babyclaude was blocked by permission prompts on the merged file.
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; methodology default after batches 1-3 precedent). T7 owns README.md, no conflicts. The SKILL.md fix-up commit landed directly on main rather than via worktree because (a) the file was already merged on main, (b) babyclaude attempts to Edit/Write got permission-denied, (c) the orchestrator-direct fix-up was a tightly-scoped 3-edit surgical pass equivalent to what babyclaude was instructed to do.

decisions.decision_text: Ready to Ship — unlock_ship flipped to true; verified_commit_sha recorded; manifest generated
decisions.rationale_text: Verification Report (verbatim summary): Phase 1 8/8 adapted plugin checks PASS (plugin.json validity, frontmatter shape across all 4 new files, R-4 grep clean 3-of-3 with 0 drift, Klaus-trio byte-identical to pre-Aron baseline via git hash-object, corpus literal-text byte-match present in both corpus-contradiction files, SKILL v1.0.1 patches present at L4/L49/L215/L244); Phase 2 catastrophiser-equivalent T8b PASS (Klaus-as-Aron audit of hidden-assumption.md hit 5 of 5 expected findings vs target ≥2, all 8 phases executed end-to-end, 16 CQs fired across 3 arguments and 4 Walton scheme assignments, 4 Pollock-classified rebuttals 1 rebut + 3 undercuts, Aron persona compliance maintained throughout, methodology survived degraded mode); Phase 3 cynic polish on skill body running in background as optional non-gating finalisation. Deferred items: 4 of 7 T8b methodology gaps to v1.1, T6 4 minor polish nits, T9 3 minor polish nits, worktree cleanup. All deferred items are documented in .claude/reviews/code/{4,6,9}.json and the Tier-1 log; none are shipping blockers. The Aron quartet (agent + skill + 2 commands + 4 references + 10 fixtures + README addition) ships independently of Klaus trio which remains byte-identical to baseline.
decisions.notes: STACK match driven by version tokens (v1.0.1, v1.1); semantically this is a verify-gate decision and a reviewer may prefer to re-route to PURPOSE or TRADEOFFS during individual escalation.

decisions.decision_text: Direct push to remote main (no PR); preserve full 24-commit history including task merges, fix-ups, cynic polish, and skill v1.0.1
decisions.rationale_text: (no annotation; selected from option list). Solo-owned plugin (single maintainer); no parallel contributors needing the PR review gate. Full per-task history is the Tier-1 audit trail's commit-level companion and is best preserved. The PR-gate methodology rule is explicitly waived for solo plugin maintenance; this decision is recorded as the architectural precedent for future Aron-like additions to this repo.
